_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
ReceptionOfficer currently has no code-level permission restrictions. It remains purely conceptual.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The app defines **three** roles in the role switcher, but only **two** are labeled “officer”: **Reception officer** and **Population officer** (the third is **Police clerk**). Here is how the two officer roles differ — in the real-world workflow and in the current code.

## Intended workflow difference

From the project glossary and domain docs:

| | **Reception officer** | **Population officer** |
|---|---|---|
| **Code** | `OfficerRole.ReceptionOfficer` | `OfficerRole.PopulationOfficer` |
| **Purpose** | Front desk — figure out why the citizen came and route them | Full registration case work — identity, residence, address, decision |
| **Typical actions** | Determine visit reason, open/route cases | Record intake data, send to police, approve/reject/suspend, issue certificates |

In the municipal workflow (`IDEA.md`), reception happens first (“determines the reason for the visit” and directs first-time registrants to the Population/Foreigners office). Population officers then take over for identity verification and the rest of the case.

## Difference in the app today

The **Population officer** role has real permission checks. The **Reception officer** role is mostly a placeholder so far.

**Population officer only:**

- `CanApproveRegistration` is `true` only for this role:

```11:11:src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Auth/CurrentOfficer.cs
    public bool CanApproveRegistration => Role == OfficerRole.PopulationOfficer;
```

- That gates case **decisions**: approve, reject, suspend, resume, and confirm registration (handlers like `ApproveCaseHandler`, `RejectCaseHandler`, etc.).
- The **Decision** section on a case only appears when `CanApproveRegistration` is true (`CaseDecisionSection.razor`).
- The **Outbound notifications** nav item is restricted to Population officers (`MainLayout.razor`).

**Reception officer:**

- No handler or UI checks reference `ReceptionOfficer` anywhere except the role switcher and display name (“Jean Martin (Reception)”).
- A reception officer can currently do most of the same things as a population officer **except** final decisions and outbound notifications.

**Shared access (both officer roles):**

- Home, Review dashboard, Registration cases
- Opening cases, intake steps (identity, address, documents, etc.)
- Police verification list is visible, but recording results is restricted to **Police clerk**, not either officer role

## Summary

- **Conceptually:** Reception = front desk routing; Population = end-to-end registration and official decisions.
- **In code:** Population officer is the only role that can approve/reject/suspend cases and see outbound notifications. Reception officer exists in the enum but has no exclusive permissions yet — it reflects the intended future “Reception” bounded context more than a enforced restriction today.

If you meant a different pair (e.g. **Population officer vs Police clerk**, or **`OfficerRole` vs `OfficerId`**), say which and I can compare those instead.
